title: eriogonum umbellatum subsp. polyanthum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. polyanthum, also known as the sulphur-flower buckwheat or Utah buckwheat, is a perennial plant native to western North America, characterized by its clusters of small, yellow flowers and a sprawling habit. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. polyanthum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. polyanthum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in traditional folk remedies for various health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
